_meta.name: analysis-artifact
_meta.status: DRAFT — Phase A / A1 (logs.md ADR-040). Field set to be validated on the drawing-samples parts during the A2 vertical slice; do not freeze before then.
_meta.role: The persistent per-file analysis artifact: what `save_analysis` writes and what the AI reads INSTEAD of re-analyzing a part. Written by the analysis pipeline (deterministic pass, optionally enriched by an AI pass per cad-planner/recipe.md); consumed by any MCP client, by rebuild_from_ir, and by the pattern matcher (A5).
_meta.location: DECIDED (user, 2026-07-03 — A1 sign-off): one artifact per source file at `<folder>/.solidpilot/<source_filename>.analysis.json`, plus a folder-level `<folder>/.solidpilot/manifest.json` (A3).
_meta.discipline: ARTIFACTS ARE A CACHE, NEVER TRUTH (source of truth = the CAD file). `identity.source_hash` + `identity.schema_version` are REQUIRED; a consumer MUST treat a hash mismatch as STALE and re-analyze. Schema evolution = REGENERATE the artifact, never migrate it (ADR-040). The `ir` block describes the part AS-ANALYZED — after any manual edit the hash changes and the whole artifact (incl. the IR) is stale.

artifact.recipe: object (required — see recipe_part / recipe_drawing by document_type)
artifact.parameters: array (required for parts, may be empty — see parameters)
artifact.ir: object | null (OPTIONAL — never mandatory, IR-ADR-006; but the pipeline always ATTEMPTS it and records the outcome, even a failure, so coverage is measurable. See ir.)
artifact.notes: array of strings (skipped content, partial reads, pitfalls hit during analysis)

identity.source_path: string (required — absolute path of the CAD file at analysis time)
identity.source_hash: string (required — 'sha256:<hex>' of the file BYTES; THE staleness key)
identity.source_mtime: string (ISO-8601 — informational only; hash decides staleness)
identity.schema_version: string (required — the version of THIS contract the artifact conforms to, e.g. '0.1.0-draft')
identity.analyzed_at: string (required — ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the analysis run)
identity.generator: object { kind: 'deterministic' | 'ai', model: 'string (only when kind=ai, e.g. an LLM id)', recipe_version: 'string (cad-planner/recipe.md version used, when an AI pass ran)' }

recipe_part._note: The canonical feature-level recipe — the same compact form `analyze_model(features)` returns (tree ORDER preserved — order is significant, never reorder on rebuild; `suppressed` only when true; deduped+rounded driving dims; pattern semantics incl. distinct_instances/wraps; per-sketch summary with plane {ref, offset}; equations). Numbers rounded to 6dp / 1µm, SI meters.
recipe_part.features: array (required — the feature tree in EXACT tree order, analyze_model(features) recipe format)
recipe_part.geometry: object { bodies, faces, edges, vertices } (counts from analyze_model(geometry) — the topology fingerprint used by round-trip verification)
recipe_part.bbox: object { dx, dy, dz } (meters — bounding box extents; a category-clustering signal)
recipe_part.equations: array (SW equations / global variables when present — authored intent, recoverable)

recipe_drawing.views: array of { name, type, scale, pos, dims: [{name, value_si}] } (flat-pattern-view dims may read 0.0 — reader limitation; PDF is ground truth)
recipe_drawing.referenced_model: string | null (path of the model the views project — the drawing↔part edge used by relationships)

parameters._note: The LIFTED named-parameter table — the basis for variants, rebinding and pattern recognition ('same pattern, different parameters'). One entry per meaningful driving dimension.
parameters.entry: { name: 'string (FULL dimension name, e.g. D1@Boss-Extrude1@Part1.Part — the modify_dimension target)', value_si: 'number (meters or radians)', feature: 'string (owning feature name)', kind: 'linear | diameter | radius | angle | count' }

ir._note: OPTIONAL block. Populated by the AI (or later deterministic) pass per cad-planner/recipe.md. Trust model: 'the LLM proposes, the round-trip decides' (IR-ADR-006) — only status='verified' IR may be trusted for rebuilds/patterns.
ir.ir_schema_version: string (required — feature-graph.schema.json version the graph targets)
ir.graph: object | null (the Feature Graph IR; null when the attempt failed before producing a graph)

verification.status: string (required — 'verified' | 'unverified' | 'failed'). unverified = graph produced but round-trip not yet run; failed = round-trip ran and missed, or the attempt could not produce/build a graph.
verification.detail: object — on verified/failed-mismatch: { topology: {bodies,faces,edges,vertices} for original AND rebuilt, volume_delta_pct, surface_delta_pct, cg_delta_mm }; on failed-attempt: { reason: 'VOCABULARY_GAP | RESOLVER_GAP | BUILD_FAILED | MISMATCH', missing: ['feature types / selectors the vocabulary lacks — feeds P1.2 coverage'] }

verified_definition: DECIDED (user, 2026-07-03 — A1 sign-off): status='verified' requires (a) topology EXACT — bodies, faces, edges, vertices counts ALL equal between original and rebuilt — AND (b) |volume delta| <= 1% AND (c) |surface-area delta| <= 1%. Anything else that built = 'failed' with detail.MISMATCH. Rationale: topology-exact matches the Aşama-2 objective standard; the 1% tolerances absorb rounding (1µm grid) without letting a wrong feature slip through.

relationships_v1._note: V1 scope is DELIBERATELY limited (ADR-040): drawing↔part matching + category clusters. The mate-based assembly graph arrives in V2/B2 with a schema_version bump (regenerate).
relationships_v1.drawings: array of paths (for a part: its drawing files, matched by referenced_model or filename)
relationships_v1.model: string | null (for a drawing: the referenced model path)
relationships_v1.category: string | null (cluster label from the deterministic pass — derived from bbox/mass ratios + feature distribution, NOT from filenames)
relationships_v1.cluster_signals: object (the raw signals behind the category, e.g. { aspect: number, revolve_dominant: bool, sheet_metal: bool, hole_count: int })
